title: Moving and Copying Files
description: Learn to move and copy files between folders using drag and keyboard shortcuts.

steps.instruction: Open Finder from the Dock and navigate to the Documents folder.
steps.hint: Click the Finder icon in the Dock, then click 'Documents' in the sidebar.

steps.instruction: You should see Notes.txt here. Click on it to select it.
steps.hint: Single-click on Notes.txt to select it (it will highlight).
With Notes.txt selected, hold Command (⌘) and press C to copy it.

steps.instruction: Now navigate to the Desktop folder using the sidebar.
steps.hint: Click 'Desktop' in the Finder sidebar on the left.

steps.instruction: Paste the file here using Cmd+V.
steps.hint: Hold Command (⌘) and press V to paste the copied file.

steps.instruction: You can also drag files between folders. Try dragging a file from the desktop into a Finder window.
steps.hint: Click and hold on any desktop file, drag it into the Finder window, and release.
